item.title: Hello World
item.author: piljoong
item.description:
Understanding Distributed ID Generation: When You Need It, Why It’s Hard, and How Different Approaches Compare

Most systems begin with the simplest possible identifier: an auto-incrementing integer in a single relational database. For many applications, this works perfectly—reliable ordering, easy debugging, and no operational overhead. But once systems scale beyond a single database or a single region, ID generation takes on architectural significance. Ordering, routing, sharing, and evolution all become part of the equation. And the cost of changing an ID format after a system has grown is far higher than most teams expect. This article isn’t an argument that every system needs distributed IDs. Many don’t. Instead, the goal is to provide a clear, honest framework for understanding:
